purpose: Tracks architecture reference exhibits, prototype files, adopter files, candidate status, and back-propagation obligations so migrations stay aligned with the latest pattern.

patterns.name: Feature screen route boundary

adopters.role: reference route screen: provider watch, auth-resolution and organizer-authority fail-closed branches, resolved viewer availability, loaded Scaffold, bottom navigation, role-correct organizer route callbacks, mutation listeners, EventDetailScreenState host/companion/booking derivation, and route side effects

provider-free EventDetailHostState, EventDetailCompanionState, and EventDetailBookingDockState adapters for club lookup display data, host message availability, companion plan availability, booking dock modes, mutation display flags, and consumer-action visibility
provider-free Event Detail display-state definitions for companion availability, host card data, host stats, section visibility, invite-loop visibility, and social/review access
reference controller seam for event-detail side effects and save mutation

embedded scroll body for reference route path; requires explicit save/share/calendar/back callbacks, companion state, host state, route-intent callbacks, and has no direct Scaffold, bottom navigation, provider reads, mutation listeners, or route side effects
provider-free host, companion, and booking dock display-state behavior coverage
canonical feature-screen inventory and screen composition map
route-state and body-state review coverage for the reference screen
adopter route screen: EventDetailViewModel watch, chromeless route Scaffold, coordinate gate, provider-free map body, and external directions callback owned by the route
provider-free display-state adapter for exact coordinate availability, map tile mode, location copy, and directions URI
focused route/body/state tests for Event Location Map screen-boundary adoption
screen-capture entries mount EventLocationMapRouteScreen for route chrome states instead of body-owned scaffold compatibility
Widgetbook route and body states use route provider overrides or explicit EventLocationMapState/callback fixtures
adopter route screen: uid/event provider waves, CalendarHomeState construction, route Scaffold, sliver scroll state, retry invalidation, and event-detail navigation callbacks
provider-free CalendarHomeState and CalendarEventSummary adapters for selected date, header mode, event merge/sort, and club-id lookup input
route shell, async/error, scroll, date selection, summary, and event-detail navigation coverage for Calendar
CalendarHomeState and CalendarEventSummary behavior coverage

adopter route screen: uid/saved-event provider wave, route Scaffold, async/error/empty states, retry invalidation, SavedEventsListState construction, club-name lookup, and saved-event detail navigation
provider-free SavedEventsListState adapter for event ordering, saved/past badge labels, tile statuses, today, and club-id lookup input
SavedEventsListState plus Saved Events route empty, ordering, and navigation coverage

adopter route screen: auth-resolution-safe initial route cache, organizer authority and app-visibility boundary, host ownership preview boundary, relationship fail-closed state, and provider-free organizer-detail content composition

adopter route screen: route-owned Explore feed/organizer/city/search/filter/source provider wave, auth-resolved account controls, guest joined-filter reset, app-browseable organizer supply, live filter-sheet count subscription, body-state branch precedence, map launcher, mutation listener, city-recovery callback, event/external navigation side effects, and provider-free chrome/body composition

adopter route screen: route-owned Explore map feed/filter provider wave, viewer-keyed cache reset, app-browseable organizer supply, map view-model construction, retry invalidation, distance-filter cycling, and event-detail navigation side effects
provider-free Explore body/sliver composer for feed async states, partial club-section failure, optional legacy club rails, activity browse callbacks, and event/external row callbacks

provider-free Explore events section shell for feed loading/error/data branch selection, empty clear/broaden callbacks, mixed-card sliver assembly, and route-owned event/external/club selection callbacks
provider-free Explore event row widgets with explicit ineligible visual weight and this-week compatibility section receiving explicit row state and route-owned event/external callbacks
provider-free Explore club card and compact club row widgets receiving explicit club data and route-owned club-selection callback
provider-free Explore event loading and empty slivers receiving explicit empty-state actions and retry/clear callbacks
provider-free Explore date strip, removable applied-filter row, and filter sheet receiving explicit date/filter/sheet state plus route-owned open and mutation callbacks
provider-free Explore browse header and discovery cover header receiving explicit search text, featured item, and route-owned search/event callbacks
Explore route/body/chrome provider-boundary coverage including provider-free body, events, filter, header fixtures plus route-level provider override tests

defined host workspace variant: canonical route aliases, uid/club/event provider wave, access gate, loaded Setup/Guests/Live/Report workspace, roster/private-access/Event Success/report/action section composition

variantPatterns.name: Host workspace route boundary
variantPatterns.exitCriteria: Promote Host Event Manage from defined variant to aligned adopter only when the loaded workspace is provider-free for selected section, roster, private access, invite links, Event Success, report, host action display data, mutation display state, retry intents, navigation, and typed callbacks while HostEventManageRouteScreen keeps canonical route aliases plus route loading, missing-resource, error, and access gates. As of 2026-07-03 HostEventManageScreenState is provider-free, but HostEventManageScreen still owns loaded-workspace provider watches and HostEventAttendancePanel still has WidgetRef-parameter debt, so the variant remains open.

variantPatterns.name: Explore provider-island compatibility variants
variantPatterns.exitCriteria: Delete ExploreList if it remains only a legacy fixture/compatibility sliver, or migrate it to the route-owned ExploreBody contract. Promote ExplorePeekRail only when the map sheet lead is either deleted as non-production compatibility code or receives feed async state, retry, analytics, and event navigation callbacks from a route/screen boundary. ExploreCityPicker was promoted after ExploreScreen/ExploreChrome took over city-list and selected-city provider reads plus typed city callbacks.

variantPatterns.name: Event Success staged screen boundary
variantPatterns.exitCriteria: Promote Event Success only after companion and host route screens are thin provider edges, display-state derivation lives in registered *_screen_state.dart adapters, provider-free part widgets remain render-only, and tests cover the host and companion screen-state adapters. The companion screen-state adapter, host screen-state adapter, and live reveal action-state adapter are aligned as of 2026-07-03; route-level ref density and route splitting remain open.

candidateQueue.reason: ChatsList still re-watches route providers for celebration and display-state construction. Keep this as explicit screen-boundary migration debt; do not mark the Chats inbox route aligned until the route owns those reads and passes display state/callbacks down.

Event Success companion now has a registered provider-free screen-state adapter, but the route screen still contains the provider wave, multiple route/render classes, and callback construction. Keep as screen-boundary debt until the route is a thin provider edge and the render surface receives explicit state/callbacks.

Event Success host now has provider-free host section/action state, but the route still owns a broad provider wave, mutation callback construction, and multiple route/render surfaces. Split the route/workspace boundary before marking the host surface aligned.

Host Operations is a 5k+ line multi-route file with multiple Consumer screens and a broad provider/ref surface. HostClubsScreenState and HostClubInsightsState are now extracted, but the file still needs route/workspace splits before applying screen-boundary adoption claims.

HostEventAttendancePanel remains a large widget with WidgetRef parameter debt and should receive route-owned attendance display state, mutation intents, and callbacks before the Host Event Manage workspace can be promoted.

patterns.requiredBackPropagation: When this exhibit changes, inspect each aligned adopter and every variant path above; update EventDetailScreen/EventDetailBody first, then EventLocationMapRouteScreen/EventLocationMapState/EventLocationMapScreen, CalendarScreen/CalendarHomeState, SavedEventsScreen/SavedEventsListState, and HostEventManageRouteScreen/ARCH-SCREEN-001C before migrating any later screen from this pattern.
patterns.notes: This pattern defines the feature-screen boundary. EventDetailBody and EventLocationMapScreen no longer expose direct Scaffold compatibility; route chrome, bottom navigation, mutation listeners, provider reads, save/share/calendar/back side effects, companion/host/booking display state, and navigation side effects belong to their route screens. EventDetailScreen now delegates host, companion, and booking dock display-state derivation to EventDetailScreenState while EventDetailCta remains the mutation/router edge for booking actions. EventLocationMapRouteScreen, CalendarScreen, SavedEventsScreen, ExploreScreen, and ExploreMapScreen are aligned adopters outside Event Detail and use provider-free state adapters or provider-free body/chrome widgets for their loaded body data. tool/architecture/check_adopted_architecture_boundaries.mjs enforces provider/routing/data/repository-free imports for aligned lib adopters with providerFree set to true. HostEventManageRouteScreen is intentionally tracked as ARCH-SCREEN-001C because its loaded surface is a host workspace with canonical route aliases and lifecycle sections; do not force it through the Event Detail body shape before the host workspace adapter exists. ExploreCityPicker is promoted: ExploreScreen/ExploreChrome own city-list and selected-city provider reads plus typed city callbacks, while the picker renders plain ExploreCityPickerState. ExploreList and ExplorePeekRail remain explicitly tracked under ARCH-SCREEN-001D until they are deleted or migrated to route-owned state/callback seams.

patterns.name: Root screen header chrome

adopters.role: reference CatchScreenHeaderTitle and CatchScreenTopBar implementation for root-screen title typography, root app-bar wrapper configuration, search slots, leading slots, and action slots
Home tab sliver title uses CatchScreenHeaderTitle.block with compact root-screen padding and notification action slot
Activity root-like route uses CatchScreenTopBar so the title matches main tab screen typography while preserving the Mark all read action slot
Explore browse and no-cover discovery headers use CatchScreenHeaderTitle/CatchScreenTopBar with city picker leading slot and shared expanding search
Chats/Host Inbox scroll-owned browse header uses CatchScreenTopBar with root title, subtitle, expanding search, and optional host filter row
Profile tab sliver title uses CatchScreenHeaderTitle.block with the settings icon in the action slot before the pinned tab rail
Dashboard header review state now mounts CatchScreenHeaderTitle instead of a one-off DashboardHeaderContent wrapper
Profile header review states now mount CatchScreenHeaderTitle instead of a one-off ProfileTitle wrapper

retiredSymbols.reason: One-off wrapper duplicated root-screen title typography and action layout now owned by CatchScreenHeaderTitle.
One-off wrapper duplicated root-screen title typography and action layout now owned by CatchScreenHeaderTitle.

patterns.requiredBackPropagation: When root-screen header typography, padding, action slots, or search composition changes, inspect every adopter above and keep Settings/Activity aligned with the five main tab surfaces. Compact/detail CatchTopBar(title: ...) screens remain outside this pattern unless product promotes them to root-like destinations.
requiredChecks: flutter analyze --no-fatal-infos lib/core/widgets/catch_top_bar.dart lib/dashboard/presentation/dashboard_home_screen.dart lib/dashboard/presentation/activity_screen.dart lib/explore/presentation/widgets/explore_header.dart lib/chats/presentation/inbox/widgets/chats_sliver_header.dart lib/user_profile/presentation/profile_screen.dart lib/safety/presentation/settings_screen.dart test/core/catch_top_bar_test.dart test/explore/explore_widgets_test.dart test/chats/chat_inbox_screen_test.dart test/profile/profile_widgets_test.dart widgetbook/lib/dashboard/dashboard_use_cases.dart widgetbook/lib/profiles/profile_use_cases.dart
patterns.notes: This pattern separates root screen voice chrome from compact navigation chrome. CatchScreenHeaderTitle owns Archivo headline typography for root titles plus optional mono eyebrow, subtitle, leading, action, material, and padding slots. CatchScreenTopBar wraps CatchTopBar when a root-like app bar also needs safe-area, action, or expanding-search behavior. Do not recreate feature-local title widgets for Home, Explore, Chats, Profile, Activity, or Settings; pass feature data and actions into the shared header slots. The screen-top-bar contract gate inventories every compact and identity app bar, exact raw media-hero exception, feature-owned Screen/Scaffold/Header/TopBar/HeaderContent class including delegated State bodies, and shell-covering editor launcher. Every discovered surface in an aligned adopter path must retain a canonical root owner, so one correct header cannot hide a second drifting implementation.

patterns.name: Pushed route chrome

adopters.role: consumer pushed list with one title hierarchy and scroll-derived divider
consumer pushed list with compact back chrome and scroll-derived divider
consumer pushed history route sharing the same empty and scroll chrome
consumer pushed settings route with scroll-derived divider
shared Consumer and Host chat detail with canonical route surface and divider
Host event workspace with scroll-derived compact chrome
Host event editor with scroll-derived compact chrome
Host loading route retaining the canonical surface and top bar
Host team workspace with canonical route surface and scroll-derived divider
Host organizer settings spokes with canonical route surface and scroll-derived divider
Host organizer step-flow loading state retaining the canonical step header

patterns.requiredBackPropagation: When pushed-route surface, divider, title, or back semantics change, update CatchRouteScaffold, every aligned Consumer and Host adopter, the top-bar manifest, focused widget tests, and the regression ledger together.
requiredChecks: flutter test test/core/catch_top_bar_test.dart test/events/saved_events_screen_test.dart test/reviews/reviews_widgets_test.dart test/payments/payment_history_screen_test.dart test/safety/settings_screen_test.dart test/chats/chat_screen_test.dart
patterns.notes: Root tab titles remain scroll content under ARCH-SCREEN-CHROME-001. This exhibit owns pushed-route surface and divider behavior only.

patterns.requiredBackPropagation: When this exhibit changes, inspect every adopter above and either update it in the same pass or mark it variant_needed/exception with a stable debt id.
requiredChecks: flutter test test/profile/profile_widgets_test.dart --plain-name 'text edit failure keeps the inline editor open with an error'
patterns.notes: This is a narrow display-state reference. CalendarHomeState remains the reference. CatchAsyncState is the provider-free async branch primitive for route-edge translations. EventDetailHostState, EventDetailCompanionState, and EventDetailBookingDockState are aligned small adapters for host-card, companion entry, and booking dock display data; EventDetailCta keeps provider mutations and router side effects at the edge. CatchesEventScreenState is aligned for queue provider-wave mapping, empty/access copy, and active deck display data; SwipeScreen now owns CatchesProfileReviewActionState for write-pending/failure display while the remaining Catches Event work is capture/reference coverage. HostClubDetailScreenState and ClubDetailBodyState are aligned for Club Detail route async branch mapping, live data, initial route fallback, host public-preview mode, next-event selection, contact actions, host-message eligibility, event-route target, review visibility, membership dock state, and retry typing; capture work remains open under the Club Detail screen contract. ExploreFeedSectionState, ExploreCoverStoryState, ExploreChromeState, ExploreEventRowState, ExploreExternalEventRowState, ExploreClubCardState, ExploreMapLauncherState, ExploreCityPickerState, ExploreCityTriggerState, ExploreFilterRailState, ExploreFilterSheetState, ExploreCollapsedMapSummaryState, ExplorePeekRailState, ExploreMapEventTicketState, ExploreScreenBodyState, ExploreDiscoveryEmptyState, and ExploreEventsEmptyState are aligned adopters for mixed-feed planning, cover-story/search-open/event-row/external-row/club-card/map-sheet/filter/chrome display data, route/body branch, city picker availability, map launcher, and empty-state actions. EventSuccessRevealActionState is aligned as a provider-free live reveal action-state adapter after the host route now translates mutation state into plain pending/error snapshots. SelfProfileScreenState and SelfProfileEditTabState are aligned provider-free Profile adopters after the route provider split and photo intent-factory rename. ChatsListScreenState, HostChatScreenState, ChatThreadLookupState, ChatReadMarkerState, and ChatRouteState are aligned provider-free Chats adopters after the route view-model split and batched inbox roster lookups. The main Explore route/feed body migration is tracked under ARCH-SCREEN-001; only the explicitly recorded ARCH-SCREEN-001D provider islands remain. The Chats inbox widget-level route reads are recorded as ARCH-SCREEN-001-CHATS-INBOX-BOUNDARY candidate debt, not as aligned screen-boundary adoption.

patterns.name: Installable app target resolution

adopters.role: six explicit role and environment app-target flavors
manifest-generated Apple target configurations with role-specific entitlements
manifest-resolved Firebase app activation
role-prefixed compatibility and store policy
approval-free Consumer and Host matrix for TestFlight and Play internal artifacts
signed Android bundle certificate, compiled role, Firebase, Maps, debug, package, and version receipt gate
Apple build-floor preflight and exact processing receipt gate
cross-run TestFlight receipt provenance gate before legacy owner retirement

exceptions.reason: GitHub is canonical for both iOS roles, both exact builds processed, and both legacy Xcode Cloud workflows are disabled. Intended TestFlight group assignment plus install and launch proof remains external.
Both signed AAB lanes are implemented, but Play records, app signing, publisher authorization, tester configuration, and install proof remain external.

patterns.requiredBackPropagation: When target fields or resolver output change, regenerate Apple configuration, inspect Android/Firebase/build wrappers and both CI build paths, then rerun the app-target gate.
requiredChecks: node tool/run.mjs check platform:app-targets platform:mobile-build-number platform:verify-ios-release-identity platform:verify-app-store-build platform:verify-ios-processing-receipts platform:verify-android-release-identity platform:probe-google-play-access
patterns.notes: The target contract is authoritative for product identity. The Flutter package and Apple Runner target may remain shared only while role-specific composition and capabilities are explicit and checked.

consumer payment status localization adapter

shared semantic exception-code localization at widget build time
consumer analytics copy adapter with explicit AppLocalizations injection
consumer semantic profile-quality suggestion adapter
marketing-owned synchronous Event Success, profile-validation, and event-policy content source
consumer and Host external-share copy with explicit AppLocalizations injection
consumer presentation-state copy resolved from AppLocalizations

patterns.requiredBackPropagation: When the copy access contract changes, update the ARB metadata validator, Flutter ownership scanner, both app-role roots, native and notification sync lanes, reference adopters, and this exhibit before migrating another slice.
patterns.notes: The ARB, native JSON, and notification JSON files are the editable product sources. Generated Dart, plist strings, and TypeScript files are consumers and must not be edited by hand.

patterns.name: Adaptive tab-root scroll clearance

adopters.role: consumer shell delegates adaptive placement and raw exclusion publication
host shell delegates the same adaptive placement contract
consumer Home populated root terminal
consumer Explore feed terminal while map overlay keeps SafeArea-relative clearance
consumer Chats root terminal
consumer Profile delegates per-tab page owners and terminals to the shared tabbed shell while retaining its feature-specific Preview scroll bridge
host Today and Events ListView terminals
host Inbox sliver terminal
host Clubs uses per-club, per-page keyed scroll owners and terminals, including the embedded consumer Club Detail preview

patterns.requiredBackPropagation: When shell placement, tab geometry, root routes, or terminal primitives change, update both shells, every manifest owner, the architecture exhibit, Widgetbook platform states, and the known-bad scanner fixtures in one pass.
patterns.notes: The shell publishes placement; root scroll primitives consume it. Android anchored chrome is already in layout, iOS floating chrome is a raw exclusion, and transient no-bar shells fall back to device safe area.

patterns.name: Route-owned nested tab screen shell

adopters.role: reference three-page adopter with a route-owned typed controller, native paging, shared page scroll owners, and a feature-owned two-way Preview scroll bridge
aligned four-page adopter with independent per-club offsets and a direct embedded read-only Club Detail preview
sliver-native consumer Club Detail adapter that keeps route chrome and interactions outside the embedded Host Preview page

retiredSymbols.reason: The feature-local overlap and focus wrapper is now the shared CatchTabbedPageScrollView.
The second-click preview adapter was replaced by direct composition of the canonical consumer Club Detail slivers.

patterns.requiredBackPropagation: When nested title, pinned rail, paging, overlap, focus, restoration, terminal, content-width, or embedded-preview policy changes, inspect Profile and Host Clubs together, keep typed controllers route-owned, preserve direct narrow-width slivers and full-bleed previews, and update the component and scroll contracts in the same pass.
patterns.notes: The shared shell owns composition mechanics only. Feature state, async policy, callbacks, and specialized inner-scroll behavior remain feature-owned. Optional content-width constraining centers the canonical 600 px lane plus page gutters only when surplus cross-axis space exists; phone layouts stay direct. Embedded detail previews reuse canonical full-bleed slivers and disable actions at one boundary instead of cloning the public screen.

patterns.name: Feature-owned startup orchestration

adopters.role: reference feature-owned keepAlive startup provider that combines current-profile state with feature-neutral device-location and city services before writing through the owning repository
composition root that activates the public feature-owned initializer without owning profile persistence or location policy
provider-container proof that app-lifetime orchestration resolves passive location and persists it through the feature repository

patterns.requiredBackPropagation: When a core provider begins watching feature data, move the orchestration to the owning feature or introduce a feature-neutral inversion contract; update the provider graph review, exhibit, and focused provider-container test in the same pass.
patterns.notes: The app root may activate feature startup work. It must not make core own a feature repository or profile stream.

patterns.name: Generated auto-dispose provider family

adopters.role: reference generated Future provider family keyed by a value-equality club-id query
generated auto-dispose Stream provider for the Event Success companion clock
generated Future provider family keyed by a value-equality Explore recommendation query
generated Future provider family keyed by a value-equality batched public-profile query
ProviderContainer proof that equivalent query keys and generated override contracts remain stable across all four adopters

exceptions.reason: _hostClubsForUserProvider remains the reviewed private part-file adapter exception recorded in provider_graph_reviews.json.

patterns.requiredBackPropagation: When a handwritten feature-owned async provider family is migrated, preserve its provider name, family call shape, value-equality key semantics, lifecycle, .future access, and override contracts; regenerate adjacent output and remove the matching planned manual-provider review decision.
patterns.notes: Generated function providers are auto-dispose by default. KeepAlive remains an explicit reviewed lifecycle decision with a rationale marker.

patterns.name: Typed form row orchestration

adopters.role: reference About You section adopter with one typed UpdateUserProfilePatch save delegate and shared accordion wiring
reference feature adapter for read, text, choice, multi-choice, and custom height descriptors without moving profile policy into core
typed descriptor, shared accordion, and single save-delegate contract coverage
Host Club Identity and Contact adopter with typed UpdateClubPatch descriptors, schema-derived constraints, and one save delegate
consumer and Host descriptor-to-schema alignment gate with a seeded drift fixture

candidateQueue.reason: Running and Lifestyle intentionally retain SelfProfile descriptors until the About You prototype receives owner API review.
Onboarding adopts opportunistically only after the reference prototype is approved.

patterns.requiredBackPropagation: When descriptor fields, save semantics, accordion ownership, or error mapping change, update the About You prototype, core descriptor tests, architecture exhibit, and every promoted adopter before broad rollout.
patterns.notes: Only the consumer Profile About You section is promoted. CatchFormCustomRow keeps the profile height stepper feature-owned while routing accordion and save behavior through CatchFormRowScope.

patterns.name: Bounded cursor read window

adopters.role: reference internal discovery page retaining a raw document cursor through exact availability and distance post-filtering
reference external discovery page sharing the same bounded Firestore window contract

consumer composition that preserves cursor exhaustiveness, load-more state, one shared date-strip query window and clock snapshot, per-date supply density, honest result count, and coordinate-complete map count
bounded newest-message stream plus explicit older-message cursor page
two-branch participant query with a compound cursor and branch-exhaustion state
bounded club, event, and reviewer streams with document-cursor history pages
bounded visible activity stream plus document-cursor history page
bounded newest-payment stream plus document-cursor history page

patterns.requiredBackPropagation: When cursor, page-size, post-filter, refresh, or count-truth semantics change, update the shared cursor helper, Explore reference controller, read-path contract, tracker adopters, focused tests, and read-limit/index scanners in the same pass.
patterns.notes: The Explore query pair is the reference accumulated feed. Realtime first-page history adopters remain bounded; documented operational-set exceptions are capped but intentionally do not expose archive pagination.

patterns.name: Pending request snapshot integrity

adopters.role: reference controller deduplicating one Firebase verification request, freezing request-defining country state, and rejecting stale callbacks after flow reset
reference frozen-snapshot form disabling phone and country controls while the submitted request is pending
controller proof for duplicate-request deduplication, immutable submitted values, and stale callback rejection
widget proof that request-defining phone and country controls stay disabled and cannot launch a second request
frozen-snapshot adopter across booking identity, prompt completion, and running preferences with route back freezing, request-control disabling, error-reset guards, and controller deduplication
controller, flow-state, and widget proof for immutable pending identity, prompt, and running-preference snapshots
frozen-snapshot adopter disabling route exit, close, reset, age, gender, and apply controls during save
controller defense returning one in-flight future for duplicate filter saves
controller and route proof for one persisted request and frozen filter controls
frozen-snapshot adopter across organizer submit, draft save, and draft restore with route, step, media, form, and default-decision freezing plus controller deduplication
controller and widget proof for one organizer submission and a frozen create route
frozen-snapshot adopter disabling route exit and the full editable event form while save is pending
controller, state, and widget proof for one event update and a frozen edit route
frozen-snapshot adopter disabling rating, comment, submit, delete, and sheet dismissal across review writes with controller deduplication
controller and widget proof for one review write and a frozen edit-sheet snapshot

whole-surface exclusive adopter freezing route, recovery, navigation, external, preference, unblock, delete, and sign-out actions during any settings mutation with settings-write and sign-out controller deduplication
state, controller, and widget proof for global settings exclusivity, one overlapping settings write, and one sign-out operation
marketing frozen-snapshot adopters disabling request-defining form, step, auth, sibling-form, shared-link, and browser-exit controls with controller single-flight guards
controller and UI proof for immutable marketing form snapshots, one request per submit, frozen auth and steps, and blocked route exits
Admin whole-surface exclusive operation lease freezing workspace controls, shared links, route navigation, peer operations, and browser unload until settlement

adopters.path: admin/src/features/access, admin/src/features/admin-roles, admin/src/features/events, admin/src/features/intake, admin/src/features/marketing, admin/src/features/organizers, admin/src/features/safety, and admin/src/features/users
adopters.role: Admin controller adopters acquiring one synchronous global lease around immutable mutation or submitted-query snapshots
cross-controller, visible-snapshot, navigation, unload, and compiled pending-action proof for the Admin exclusive policy

variantPatterns.name: Versioned editable snapshot
variantPatterns.rule: Keep edits live only with an immutable request generation and stale-result rejection or cancellation plus explicit queued-or-next-submit UI.

variantPatterns.name: Independently keyed concurrency
variantPatterns.rule: Allow simultaneous operations only when typed scope keys and separate pending/error surfaces prevent sibling interference.

patterns.requiredBackPropagation: When pending ownership, request-defining inputs, reset behavior, cancellation, deduplication, route exits, scope keys, or stale-result handling change, update the Phone Authentication reference, every promoted adopter, feature-contract action matrices, screen debt, regression guard, and focused interaction tests in the same pass.
requiredChecks: npm --prefix website exec vitest run -- --project=unit src/features/waitlist/useWaitlistFormController.test.tsx src/features/host/application/useHostApplicationController.test.tsx src/features/claims/useClaimFlowController.test.tsx src/features/claims/useClaimRequestMutation.test.tsx src/shared/pendingRequest.test.tsx
patterns.notes: Frozen snapshot is the default for one submitted form or route action. Versioned editing and independently keyed concurrency are explicit variants, not implicit exceptions. Phone Authentication is the reference; Member Onboarding, Matching Preferences, Host Organizer Create, Host Event Edit, Reviews, Account Settings, Marketing forms, and the Admin console are aligned adopters. Admin deliberately uses one global exclusive lease because operator writes and submitted queries share navigation, feedback, query-cache, and selection surfaces; the small throughput trade-off prevents cross-workspace ambiguity.

patterns.name: App router and deterministic integration lifecycle

adopters.role: data-driven app proof harness that executes every registered public-surface configuration against production route, organizer authority, Explore account-control, event-availability, and action-disposition helpers
focused guest, auth-resolution, booking-ready, social-ready, onboarding-resume, and host/consumer route redirect coverage
app root with synchronous owned localization delegates and component-native English fallback for the nonlocalized country picker
headless wrapper proving repeated keyed ProviderScope and GoRouter lifecycles in one deterministic process
exact macOS golden and deterministic headless app-shell CI lane

exceptions.reason: original suites remain optional native-device entrypoints when APP_SHELL_NATIVE_INTEGRATION is explicitly enabled

patterns.requiredBackPropagation: When the app root, GoRouter ownership, public route classification, integration binding, app-shell navigation, localization delegates, or mounted async behavior changes, update the public-surface matrix and harness, shared integration harness, headless wrappers, native runner, CI lane, regression ledger, and focused shell/golden coverage together.
requiredChecks: flutter test --concurrency=1 test/core/catch_primitives_test.dart --plain-name 'CatchTabBar keeps four animated destinations within 390px'
patterns.notes: Every consumer route is owned by exactly one public-surface matrix class even when guest tab chrome hides it. Consumer integration cases cover consumer-owned routes only. Host creation and attendance remain in host surface/controller tests; Dashboard recommendations remain owned by Explore.
